message:
test(charmed): demo showcase app and test infrastructure updates

App Module:
- Improve demo_showcase app.rs structure
- Better test integration support

Test Support:
- Enhance test_support.rs with additional utilities
- Add new test helpers

Test Directory:
- Add dedicated tests/ directory for demo_showcase
- Organize test fixtures

Sync beads issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -21,7 +21,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1cgb","title":"EPIC: E2E Test Coverage with Detailed Logging","description":"End-to-end tests for complete application workflows with comprehensive logging.

## Philosophy
- Test full user workflows, not just units
- Detailed logging for debugging test failures
- Capture terminal state at each step
- Support both headless and interactive modes

## Target Applications
1. demo_showcase - primary E2E target
   - All 8 pages (Dashboard, Services, Jobs, Logs, Docs, Files, Wizard, Settings)
   - Navigation flows (sidebar, keyboard shortcuts)
   - Theme switching and persistence
   - Shell-out and restore patterns
   - Wizard deployment workflow

2. glow - markdown viewer
   - File loading and rendering
   - Navigation and search
   - Stash operations

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamp every test step
- Log all key presses and expected outcomes
- Capture full terminal state on failure
- Include ANSI sequence dumps for debugging
- Log timing information for performance tests","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:54.027535577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:55.306157878Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1cgb","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:54.034668586Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-1cgb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"E2E LOGGING REQUIREMENTS - Non-Negotiable:

Every E2E test MUST include detailed logging that makes debugging failures possible
without re-running the test interactively. This is critical for CI/CD reliability.

REQUIRED LOG CONTENT:
1. Test identification (name, timestamp, environment)
2. Each test step with description
3. All input events (key presses, mouse clicks)
4. Expected outcome for each assertion
5. Actual outcome (pass/fail with details)
6. Terminal state capture on failure
7. Timing information for performance analysis

LOG FORMAT EXAMPLE:
```
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.123Z] TEST_START: test_wizard_deployment_flow
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.124Z] ENV: TERM=xterm-256color, COLORTERM=truecolor
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.125Z] STEP: Initialize application
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.130Z] STEP: Navigate to Wizard page (key: '7')
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.131Z] INPUT: KeyEvent { code: Char('7'), modifiers: NONE }
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.135Z] ASSERT: current_page == Page::Wizard
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.135Z] RESULT: PASS
[2026-01-28T12:00:00.136Z] STEP: Enter service name 'test-service'
...
```

The infrastructure task (bd-28kk) MUST be completed first - all other E2E tests
depend on it for consistent logging.","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dr6","title":"demo_showcase page: Jobs/Tasks (async workflows, progress, filtering)","description":"A realistic CI/job-runner screen: a dense jobs list/table with progress, filtering, and actions that feel like a real ops console.

Why:
- This page is the best “integration crucible” for the stack:
  - bubbletea message routing + command composition
  - bubbles components (table/list, progress, spinner, paginator, timer/stopwatch)
  - harmonica motion for compelling progress/counter animation (optional)
  - charmed_log/toasts for outcomes and errors
- It proves the architecture scales beyond toy demos.

Must showcase (minimum):
- Navigation + selection:
  - keyboard-first (arrows/jk, enter), consistent focus model
  - selection stability across refreshes and filter changes
- Status + progress:
  - determinate progress bars and indeterminate spinners
  - color-coded statuses (running/succeeded/failed/cancelled)
  - durations + ETA-ish hints (timer/stopwatch)
- Realistic operations:
  - start/cancel/retry actions implemented via Cmd/AsyncCmd
  - action results visible (toast + log + UI state)
- Discoverability:
  - query bar + filtering + sorting
  - paginator for large lists
- Details pane:
  - shows job metadata, last logs, parameters, timeline / steps

Data requirements:
- Driven by the deterministic simulation engine and seeded generator.
- Integrates with Wizard: wizard triggers new jobs; jobs correlate to logs.

Acceptance:
- Feels like an actual CI dashboard, not a toy.
- Actions are responsive: input never blocks, state updates are obvious.
- Filtering is fast and predictable.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - filtering/sorting correctness and stability
  - action state machine (start/cancel/retry) transitions
  - progress rendering decisions (spinner vs bar, colors)
- E2E:
  - wizard -> job -> logs correlation scenario
  - smoke tour coverage
  - optional “jobs filtering” scenario if needed for regression.

Notes:
- Keep the information density high, but never sacrifice clarity.
- Make sure `--no-animations` still looks good (no “blank” experience).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:17.395269743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:41:41.424387508Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dr6","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:17.423951863Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fxb","title":"Logs: filtering + search (level/service/query)","description":"Add filtering + search controls to the Logs page.

Why:
- Logs are only useful when you can slice them quickly.
- This is also a great way to showcase bubbles textinput + the app’s focus/keybinding model.

Filters to implement:
- Level selector (e.g., TRACE/DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR)
- Service selector (from demo domain model)
- Free-text query (substring or simple matcher)

Performance requirements:
- Filtering should feel instant.
- Avoid re-rendering/parsing the entire log history on every keystroke.
- Prefer an incremental or pre-indexed filter model that can be unit tested.

UX/polish:
- Highlight matches in the viewport (optional).
- Bracketed paste should work in the query input.
- Show an inline summary: "123 lines (12 matches)".

Acceptance:
- Filtering feels instant and scales to a large buffer.
- Covered by unit tests at the filtering-engine layer.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:38.782877999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:55:30.352469043Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:55:30.352332649Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logs","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:38.799160009Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-3jg2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:41.631552397Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxb","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:39.044747386Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1fxl","title":"Interactions: focus/blur awareness","description":"Use `FocusMsg`/`BlurMsg` to improve UX and avoid wasting resources when the terminal is unfocused.

Why:
- Focus reporting is an advanced terminal feature that bubbletea supports; the demo should prove it.
- Many TUIs burn CPU animating off-screen. We should behave like a polished app.

Behavior:
- Visual indicator:
  - show a subtle "unfocused" state in the header/status bar (icon + text)
  - reduce contrast of animated/active elements when unfocused
- Optional resource savings:
  - pause or throttle animations
  - pause or slow the background simulation tick

Constraints:
- Must not break determinism in tests:
  - focus/blur should be injectable as messages
  - headless runs should default to focused unless scenario changes it

Acceptance:
- App clearly communicates focus state.
- Unfocused mode avoids needless animation work without breaking the user’s mental model.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.412081335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:51:10.380601268Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","focus","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.434141459Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:59.432221685Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:00.706143825Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1fxl","title":"Interactions: focus/blur awareness","description":"Use `FocusMsg`/`BlurMsg` to improve UX and avoid wasting resources when the terminal is unfocused.

Why:
- Focus reporting is an advanced terminal feature that bubbletea supports; the demo should prove it.
- Many TUIs burn CPU animating off-screen. We should behave like a polished app.

Behavior:
- Visual indicator:
  - show a subtle "unfocused" state in the header/status bar (icon + text)
  - reduce contrast of animated/active elements when unfocused
- Optional resource savings:
  - pause or throttle animations
  - pause or slow the background simulation tick

Constraints:
- Must not break determinism in tests:
  - focus/blur should be injectable as messages
  - headless runs should default to focused unless scenario changes it

Acceptance:
- App clearly communicates focus state.
- Unfocused mode avoids needless animation work without breaking the user’s mental model.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.412081335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:55:00.660400674Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:55:00.660271694Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","focus","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.434141459Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:59.432221685Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:00.706143825Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1g80","title":"E2E scenario: file picker navigate + preview","description":"Add an end-to-end scenario that exercises the Files page (bubbles FilePicker + preview).

Scenario outline:
1) Launch demo_showcase in deterministic headless mode.
2) Navigate to Files.
3) Use the file picker to:
   - move selection
   - enter a directory (if supported)
   - select a file
4) Validate preview behavior:
   - preview pane becomes non-empty
   - breadcrumb / filename reflects the selected entry
   - selecting a markdown fixture renders via glamour (not raw markdown), using the current theme

Notes:
- Avoid relying on the host filesystem. Use the demo’s deterministic fixture directory (from the asset strategy / Config).

Acceptance:
- Deterministic in CI.
- Failure artifacts include the directory listing, selected filename, and the last rendered Files frame.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyLantern","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.191594688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:06.564367389Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:54:06.564301366Z","close_reason":"Integrated Files page into app (Page enum, Pages struct, routing). Added E2E tests for file picker navigation and preview functionality. Tests verify: fixture mode, j/k navigation, Enter to select/enter, Backspace to go back, and hidden file toggle.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","files","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-1wtl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.230688217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-22c0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:07:58.608108725Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.221867959Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.217633407Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.226320857Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g80","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:27.213212187Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gdn","title":"Add lipgloss individual border edge colors and Transform method","description":"Add per-edge border colors and Transform method to Lipgloss.

Scope:
- Extend border style API to set color per edge (top/right/bottom/left).
- Implement Transform method for style manipulation consistent with Go (e.g., normalize or mutate styles).
- Add unit tests for per-edge colors and Transform behavior.
- Update docs if API changes are user-visible.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Per-edge border colors render correctly in multiple combinations.
- Transform method behaves as documented and has unit coverage.
- Conformance fixtures updated if needed to include per-edge color scenarios.","notes":"Files: crates/lipgloss/src/style.rs, border.rs, renderer.rs. Maintain backward compatibility where practical (but no shims).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:48.923880193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T18:15:01.451539959Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T18:15:01.450257984Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1i7e","title":"demo_showcase: CLI contract (modes, flags, help)","description":"Define the binary UX for launching the demo.

Proposed modes/flags (finalize based on UX concept):
- default: run interactive local TUI
- `--theme <name|auto>`
- `--theme-file <path>` (optional)
- `--seed <u64>` (stable demo data)
- `--no-animations`
- `--no-mouse`
- `--no-color` (force ASCII)
- `--no-alt-screen` (run without alt-screen; useful for debugging + demonstrating bubbletea println/printf)
- `--self-check` (headless CI-friendly render + exit)

Optional subcommands:
- `ssh --addr :2222 --host-key <path>` (wish integration; feature-gated)
- `export --html <path>` / `export --plain <path>` (optional: export snapshots)

Acceptance:
- `--help` is clean and self-explanatory.
- Flags map directly to `demo_showcase::Config` fields.
- Error messages are user-friendly (bad theme name, bad seed, missing file).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.810927082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539935175Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539870655Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:05.884573066Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-szmb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.830556320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1p61","title":"Unit tests: domain action API correctness","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for the domain action API.

Coverage:
- start job -> job exists + correct initial state
- progress ticks update job correctly
- cancel job -> state transitions + logs/notifications
- retry failed job -> new attempt semantics
- deploy action -> deployment + job correlation

Logging:
- Tests should log action inputs + resulting state diffs for easy debugging.

Acceptance:
- The action API is covered with high confidence and runs fast.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:05.168723242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:24:21.174597921Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:24:21.174533320Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1p61","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:05.181377320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1p61","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:19.061233110Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1qwj","title":"demo_showcase core: notifications/toasts + status messages","description":"Add transient notifications and persistent status messaging.

Why:
- Real apps communicate state changes (saved, error, connected, job finished).

Implementation:
- Small toast stack or one-line status area.
- Uses lipgloss styles (success/warn/error) and optional harmonica animation later.

Acceptance:
- Pages can emit notifications via app messages; app renders them consistently.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:01.374394082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:10:37.971622922Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:10:37.971559794Z","close_reason":"Implemented notification/toast system with Notification struct, NotificationMsg, and rendering in footer area.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","polish","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qwj","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:01.402524089Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qwj","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:25.545146309Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qwj","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:24.293003527Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1s7t","title":"E2E Tests: Logs page filtering and search","description":"End-to-end tests for the Logs page filtering and search functionality.

## Purpose
Test the log viewer with its filtering, level selection,
and search capabilities.

## Test Requirements

### Log Display
- [ ] Logs render with timestamps
- [ ] Log levels color-coded
- [ ] Long logs truncate/wrap correctly
- [ ] Syntax highlighting in log messages

### Level Filtering
- [ ] Filter by ERROR only
- [ ] Filter by WARN and above
- [ ] Filter by INFO and above
- [ ] Filter by DEBUG and above
- [ ] Filter by TRACE (all)

### Text Search
- [ ] Search filter input works
- [ ] Search highlights matches
- [ ] Search is case-insensitive
- [ ] Clear search shows all

### Navigation
- [ ] Scroll through log list
- [ ] Jump to top/bottom
- [ ] Page up/down
- [ ] Line numbers optional

### Real-time Updates
- [ ] New logs appear at bottom
- [ ] Auto-scroll when at bottom
- [ ] Stay position when scrolled up

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log filter changes
- [ ] Log search queries
- [ ] Log scroll position changes

## Success Criteria
- Filtering works correctly
- Search finds all matches
- Large log volumes perform well","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyMeadow","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:21.701891676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:50:12.858901822Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","logs","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1s7t","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:21.720531569Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1s7t","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:10.316035449Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1s7t","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:44.463158140Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1tdh","title":"Jobs: paginator integration for long lists","description":"Add pagination for long Jobs lists using `bubbles::paginator`.

Why:
- Long lists are a common real-world case; pagination prevents the UI from becoming sluggish.
- It also showcases another Bubbles component in a realistic workflow.

Behavior:
- Paginator reflects total pages and current page.
- Works correctly with filtering/sorting:
  - applying a filter updates total pages
  - selection remains stable (best-effort) when the filtered set changes
- Keyboard:
  - next/prev page shortcuts
  - jump to first/last (optional)

Acceptance:
- Large job sets remain responsive.
- Pagination UX feels consistent with the rest of the app (styled, discoverable).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:21.084401297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:43.856579389Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","paginator"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tdh","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:21.103425435Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tdh","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:02.336132860Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1u1s","title":"Unit Tests: bubbletea core (Cmd, Message, Model)","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for bubbletea core functionality.

## Current State
bubbletea has basic tests but gaps in:
- Cmd::batch and Cmd::sequence edge cases
- Message type coercion and downcasting
- Model trait default implementations

## Test Requirements

### Cmd Module
- [ ] Cmd::new() with various closure types
- [ ] Cmd::batch() with empty, single, multiple commands
- [ ] Cmd::sequence() ordering guarantees
- [ ] Cmd::blocking() thread behavior
- [ ] Cmd cancellation (if supported)

### Message Module
- [ ] Message::new() with all message types
- [ ] Message::is::<T>() type checking
- [ ] Message::downcast::<T>() success/failure
- [ ] Message cloning and equality

### Key/Mouse Events
- [ ] KeyEvent parsing from raw bytes
- [ ] MouseEvent coordinate handling
- [ ] Modifier key combinations
- [ ] Special keys (F1-F12, arrows, etc.)

## Success Criteria
- 100% coverage of public Cmd API
- 100% coverage of Message API
- All edge cases documented with tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyLantern","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:21.686576405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:54.673408772Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:54.673341717Z","close_reason":"Added 24 comprehensive unit tests for bubbletea core: Cmd (batch/sequence edge cases, new_optional, blocking, captured values), Message (downcast_ref, Debug format, all built-in types, equality/clone/copy traits).  {"id":"bd-1wtl","title":"Files: preview pane (viewport)","description":"Add a preview pane for the selected file using `bubbles::viewport`.

Why:
- Makes the Files page feel like a real tool (inspect configs/logs/docs quickly).
- Exercises viewport scrolling + resize behavior.

Preview behavior:
- For plain text:
  - show a bounded preview (first N lines/bytes) with a "truncated" indicator
  - choose a clear wrapping strategy (wrap-by-default is fine; optionally support horizontal scroll)
- Show lightweight metadata (optional but nice):
  - file size, last modified, detected type (text/markdown/binary)
- Resize handling:
  - preview reflows correctly on window resize

Acceptance:
- Large files do not lock up the UI; preview work is bounded.
- Scrolling the preview is smooth and does not interfere with file picker navigation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:19.317694097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:11:21.579944103Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:11:21.579876477Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","files","viewport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wtl","depends_on_id":"bd-201t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:19.337282014Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1wtl","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:14.307425706Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
